title: Fragments: The Hedgehog and the Fox
description: The earliest Greek lyric poet — soldier, lover, realist — and the original source of 'the fox knows many things, the hedgehog one great thing.'
introduction: The earliest Greek lyric poet — soldier, lover, realist — and the original source of 'the fox knows many things, the hedgehog one great thing.'

translation.original_text: πόλλ᾿ οἶδ᾿ ἀλώπηξ, ἀλλ᾿ ἐχῖνος ἓν μέγα.
translation.text: The fox knows many things, but the hedgehog knows one great thing.

translation.original_text: θυμέ, θύμ᾿, ἀμηχάνοισι κήδεσιν κυκώμενε, ἄνα δέ... γίγνωσκε δ᾿ οἷος ῥυσμὸς ἀνθρώπους ἔχει.
translation.text: Heart, my heart, churned by hopeless troubles — rise up!... Recognize what rhythm holds mankind.
